heading: Renewable Energy Innovation Manufacturing Partnership
label: 17244 Renewable Energy Innovation Manufacturing Partnership
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program, to be known as the Renewable Energy Innovation Manufacturing Partnership Program (referred to in this section as the “Program”), to make assistance awards to eligible entities for use in carrying out research, development, and demonstration relating to the manufacturing of renewable energy technologies.

children.heading: Solicitation
children.label: b Solicitation
children.text: To carry out the Program, the Secretary shall annually conduct a competitive solicitation for assistance awards for an eligible project described in subsection (e).
to develop, or aid in the development of, advanced manufacturing processes, materials, and infrastructure;
to increase the domestic production of renewable energy technology and components; and
to better coordinate Federal, State, and private resources to meet regional and national renewable energy goals through advanced manufacturing partnerships.

1 or more public or private nonprofit institutions or national laboratories engaged in research, development, demonstration, or technology transfer, that would participate substantially in the project; and

1 or more private entities engaged in the manufacturing or development of renewable energy system components (including solar energy, wind energy, biomass, geothermal energy, energy storage, or fuel cells).
the conduct of studies of market opportunities for component manufacturing of renewable energy systems;
the conduct of multiyear applied research, development, demonstration, and deployment projects for advanced manufacturing processes, materials, and infrastructure for renewable energy systems; and
other similar ventures, as approved by the Secretary, that promote advanced manufacturing of renewable technologies.

children.heading: Criteria and guidelines
children.label: f Criteria and guidelines
children.text: The Secretary shall establish criteria and guidelines for the submission, evaluation, and funding of proposed projects under the Program.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: g Cost sharing
children.text: shall apply to a project carried out under this section.Section 16352 of this title

children.heading: Disclosure
children.label: h Disclosure
children.text: The Secretary may, for a period of up to 5 years after an award is granted under this section, exempt from mandatory disclosure under (popularly known as the Freedom of Information Act) information that the Secretary determines would be a privileged or confidential trade secret or commercial or financial information under subsection (b)(4) of such section if the information had been obtained from a non-Government party.section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Sense of the Congress
children.label: i Sense of the Congress
children.text: It is the sense of the Congress that the Secretary should ensure that small businesses engaged in renewable manufacturing be given priority consideration for the assistance awards provided under this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: j Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated out of funds already authorized to carry out this section $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2013, to remain available until expended.
